meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve slow development speed (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome slow development speed. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Antigravity Template to Solve Slow Dev Speed
content.seo_description: Stop wasting time on boilerplate. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship production-ready SaaS apps at lightning speed.
content.answer_block: Slow development speed is usually a byproduct of manual scaffolding. By utilizing the Antigravity project template, you eliminate repetitive setup. Integrating this with Claude Code allows you to automate architectural patterns, reducing your time-to-market by 70% and ensuring your codebase remains clean, scalable, and ready for high-paying client delivery.
content.intro:
The modern freelance landscape is a war of attrition. Every hour spent wrestling with environment variables, auth boilerplate, or database schemas is an hour stolen from your bottom line. You are being paid for the value you deliver, not the hours you spend configuring webpack or fighting dependency conflicts. The friction is real, and it is the silent killer of your profitability.

But what if you could bypass the 'blank slate' phase entirely? The Antigravity project template acts as your kinetic launchpad, providing a hardened, production-ready foundation that anticipates your needs before you write a single line of business logic. When paired with the precision of Claude Code, you don't just write code—you orchestrate high-performance systems with surgical speed.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Establish the core project structure, ensuring all security headers, linting, and CI/CD pipelines are pre-configured for instant deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the 'SaaS-Starter' template. Include Stripe integration, Supabase auth, and Tailwind CSS configuration.

steps.title: Automate Domain Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map your database schema to API endpoints to avoid manual routing and CRUD boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the schema.prisma file and generate the necessary REST API endpoints and service layers to handle user-specific data retrieval.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate CRUD service layer based on schema.prisma'

steps.title: Enforce Architectural Rigor
steps.description: Inject custom design patterns into your codebase to ensure maintainability for future client hand-offs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply the repository pattern to all data-fetching modules to decouple business logic from the database implementation.

steps.title: Accelerate UI Component Generation
steps.description: Generate high-fidelity UI components that match your client's brand guidelines using Antigravity's component library.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive dashboard layout component that includes a sidebar, top navigation, and a data-table view with sorting capabilities.

steps.title: Continuous Integration & Quality Assurance
steps.description: Set up automated testing suites to ensure your rapid development doesn't compromise the stability of the application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a comprehensive end-to-end test suite for the authentication flow using Playwright, covering happy path and error states.

pro_tips: Use Claude Code's '--diff' flag to review architectural changes before they hit your main branch, preventing 'AI hallucination' drift.
Store your frequently used Antigravity prompts in a local 'prompts.json' file for consistent output across multiple client projects.
Always run 'antigravity audit' after an AI-driven refactor to identify any deprecated dependencies or security vulnerabilities introduced.
Leverage Claude Code to document your code automatically; generate JSDoc comments for all exported functions to ensure high-quality hand-offs.
Create a 'template-config.yaml' to standardize your directory structure; this allows you to swap out stacks without rewriting your prompt library.
content.next_steps: After completing your first build, document your custom prompts into a shared library to create a 'freelance flywheel' that compounds your speed with every new client project.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting slow development cycles eat your margins. With Antigravity and Claude Code, you are no longer just a coder—you are a high-velocity product engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
